Composition.Programmer 1: Fix and enhance main.py backend Flask application for EventPlanning web application. Specifically, fix the issue in the 'dashboard' function where the template file 'dashboard.html' is missing, causing jinja2.exceptions.TemplateNotFound error. Ensure the 'dashboard.html' template file is created and placed in the Flask application's templates folder with the required HTML structure and elements as specified in the requirements document, including IDs  dashboard-page, featured-events, browse-events-button, view-tickets-button, and venues-button. Also verify that all other routes and rendering calls in main.py are consistent with the requirements and templates.
Composition.Programmer 2: Implement the 'dashboard.html' template file for the EventPlanning web application. This template must include the main hub displaying upcoming events, featured venues, and quick navigation buttons to all functionalities as specified in the requirements document. The template should contain the required elements with correct IDs  dashboard-page (container div), featured-events (div for featured event recommendations), browse-events-button, view-tickets-button, and venues-button (buttons for navigation). Ensure the template is compatible with the Flask backend and uses Jinja2 templating syntax to display passed data such as upcoming events and featured venues.

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
Task 3  Test the elements and integrity of all pages. This includes verifying the presence and correctness of all specified elements on each page as per the requirements

background_prompt: EvoMAC is a software company powered by multiple intelligent agents, such as chief executive officer, chief human resources officer, chief product officer, chief technology officer, etc, with a multi-agent organizational structure and the mission of 'changing the digital world through programming'.
